title: cystopteris fragilis var. azorica
intro: Cystopteris fragilis var. azorica, also known as Azorean Hart's tongue fern, is a rare and endemic variety of the Hart's tongue fern species, characterized by its distinctive fronds and unique habitat requirements. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cystopteris fragilis var. azorica include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically to treat skin irritations and wounds.
